content.full_text:
City Of Calimesa

F. Thos. Caporael, City Attorney
1025 Calimesa Blvd., Suite 3A
Calimesa, California 92320
Telephone (714) 795-1745
Fax (714) 795-2108

September 26, 1991

Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

The City Council of the City of Calimesa is contemplating passage of a rent control ordinance to regulate the rental of spaces in mobile home parks. In my opinion, some of the councilmembers have conflicts of interests which could prohibit them from voting for or against such an ordinance, or influencing the decision of the remaining councilmembers. The rent control ordinance will, however, have a tremendous political impact because of the large number of mobile home parks in Calimesa. Pursuant to Government Code section 83114, I am, therefore, on behalf of the City Council, requesting the commission to issue an opinion on the following questions:

1. May a councilmember who resides in a mobile home park vote for or against, or influence the decision of the City Council on, a mobile home rent control ordinance?

2. May a councilmember who resides in a mobile home park under a long term (10 years) lease vote for or against, or influence the decisions of the City Council on, a mobile home rent control ordinance?

3. May a councilmember whose professional practice includes clients who reside in mobile home parks vote for or against, or influence the decision of the City Council on, a rent control ordinance?

4. May a councilmember whose parents or in-laws reside in a mobile home park vote for or against, or influence the decision of the City Council on, a rent control ordinance?

These questions do not apply to all councilmembers and section 87101 will not be applicable.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation.

Very truly yours,

F. Thos. Caporael
City Attorney

FTC/ncb
